title: The Occurrence of Peanut Mottle Virus in India
abstract: A virus causing mottle, inter venial depression and upward rolling of peanut leaf margins was found in the Punjab State.  The Pathogen was identified as peanut mottle virus (PMV) on the basis of serology, host range, aphid transmission, and physical properties in crude sap.  The occurrence of PMV has not been reported previously from India.  A highly sensitive serological methods, using hemagglutination, was used successfully to detect PMV antigens in crude extracts of peanut, soybean, and pea.
